title: Bank Privacy Reform Act
plain_summary: The Bank Privacy Reform Act would change the rules about how the government can look at people's bank records. Right now, banks must automatically report certain transactions to the government, like when someone moves more than $10,000. This bill would stop those automatic reports and instead make government agencies get a search warrant from a judge before they can see bank records. The law currently helps catch criminals who launder money or fund terrorism, but this bill would require more steps to protect people's privacy.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Bank Privacy Reform Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Bank Secrecy Act Reforms
sections.text: This section makes changes to existing banking privacy laws. It changes the Right to Financial Privacy Act of nineteen seventy eight in several ways. First, it requires government agencies to get a search warrant before they can look at or get copies of bank records from any customer. The search warrant must clearly describe what records they want. Second, it removes several sections from the current law that allowed government access to bank records without warrants. It also removes many reporting requirements that banks currently have to follow. The section also changes laws about money laundering and financial crimes. It removes requirements for banks to report certain transactions and removes special measures that can be taken against foreign banks. It keeps some basic record keeping requirements for banks but eliminates many reporting duties. The changes also adjust the dollar amount that triggers certain requirements so it goes up each year based on inflation. Many sections of current banking law are completely removed by these changes.
